item.output:
## IWSDK Build Check-in — Continuation Protocol

You are an hourly sentinel job. Your job is to check whether the overnight IWSDK build needs to continue.

### Decision logic:

1. **Read** `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/current-build-status.json`

2. **If `status` is `"building"`** → A build agent is currently running. Do nothing. Report: "Build still in progress."

3. **If `status` is `"complete"` or total_build_minutes >= 360** → The 6-hour target has been met. Do nothing. Report: "Build complete, target reached."

4. **If `status` is `"idle"` and no `date` set for today** → The midnight job hasn't run yet or failed. Do nothing. Report: "No build started today."

5. **If `status` is `"waiting_continuation"` and total_build_minutes < 360** → The previous round stopped early. You must continue building. Follow the continuation protocol below.

### ⚠️ CRITICAL: Spatial UI Only — NO HTML Overlays

**Before expanding features, audit existing code for HTML DOM UI anti-patterns.**

Search the codebase for: `document.createElement`, `innerHTML`, `<div>`, `<canvas>`, `.style.`, `document.body`, `getElementById` on `document` (not on `UIKitDocument`). If any game UI uses HTML DOM overlays, **fix them first** by converting to `.uikitml` PanelUI panels before adding new features.

All game UI must use IWSDK's PanelUI system:
- `.uikitml` templates compiled by `@iwsdk/vite-plugin-uikitml`
- `PanelUI` component for 3D panels
- `Follower` component for head-locked HUDs
- `ScreenSpace` component for browser overlays that auto-adapt in XR
- Read section **4b** of the IWSDK dev skill at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md`

### ⚠️ CRITICAL: XR Verification Gate

**You MUST verify all UI and interactions work in XR BEFORE expanding features.**

Use IWSDK CLI tools (`npx iwsdk xr`, `npx iwsdk ecs pause/step/snapshot`) to confirm:
- All PanelUI panels render and are readable in XR
- XR controller laser pointer can interact with all buttons/menus
- HUD panels follow the player's head correctly
- No HTML overlays remain in the codebase

If XR verification fails, fix the issues before adding anything else.

### Continuation protocol:

When continuing a build:

1. **Read the IWSDK dev skill** at `~/workspace/skills/iwsdk-dev/SKILL.md`

2. **Read the status file** to understand:
   - What game is being built (`game_name`, `game_dir`)
   - How many minutes have been accumulated (`total_build_minutes`)
   - What features are already done (`features_completed`)
   - How many rounds have run (`rounds`)

3. **Update status file**: Set `status` to `"building"`, set `last_updated_utc`

4. **Assess the current codebase**: Read source files, check for type errors, understand what exists

5. **Plan expansion**: Based on what's missing, plan the next set of improvements. Prioritize:
   - Fix any type errors or broken code first
   - **Convert any HTML DOM UI to PanelUI spatial panels** (this blocks everything else)
   - XR controller support (if not yet working end-to-end)
   - **Verify all UI and interactions in XR** before proceeding
   - Visual polish and new content
   - New game modes
   - Audio improvements
   - Performance optimization
   - README and documentation

6. **Build**: Implement the planned improvements. Push commits and redeploy to gh-pages at milestones.

7. **When done**: Update the status file:
   - Append new round to `rounds` array with timing and stats
   - Update `total_build_minutes`
   - Update `features_completed`
   - Set `status` to `"waiting_continuation"` if still under 6h, or `"complete"` if ≥6h
   - Set `last_updated_utc`

8. **Capture journal**: Copy your session JSONL into `.agent/journal/` in the game repo. Commit and push.

9. **Update build log and summary**: Append to `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/BUILD_LOG.md` and overwrite `~/workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary.md` with current state.

### GitHub deploy commands:
```
cd ~/workspace/<game-name>
git add -A && git commit -m "<description>" && git push origin main
npm run build
cd dist && rm -rf .git && git init && git checkout -b gh-pages && git add -A && git commit -m "Deploy"
git remote add origin https://github.com/ellyz2426/<game-name>.git
git push -f origin gh-pages
cd .. && rm -rf dist/.git
```

Note: GitHub token does NOT have `workflow` scope. Use direct gh-pages branch deployment only.

item.name: write
item.arguments:
{"content":"# Neon Breaker VR

A holodeck-style brick-breaking game built with [IWSDK](https://iwsdk.dev) (Immersive Web SDK) 0.4.1. Play in VR with XR controllers or in your browser with mouse/keyboard — same codebase, same physics, same neon aesthetic.

**[▶ Play Now](https://ellyz2426.github.io/neon-breaker/)**

## Features

### 🎮 8 Game Modes
- **Classic** — 48 handcrafted levels across 4 zones, 3 lives, full campaign progression
- **Endless** — Levels loop with escalating difficulty, no end in sight
- **Time Attack** — 90-second score sprint
- **Zen** — Unlimited lives, pure relaxation
- **Daily Challenge** — Seeded daily layout, compete against your own high score
- **Survival** — Wave-based spawning, 1 life, how long can you last?
- **Practice** — Replay any previously cleared level
- **Boss Rush** — All 4 bosses back-to-back, 2x XP rewards

### 🧱 6 Brick Types
- **Normal** — 1 hit, 100 points
- **Tough** — 2 hits, 200 points (shows visible damage on hit)
- **Armored** — 3 hits, 300 points (progressive damage visualization)
- **Explosive** — 1 hit, 150 points, chain-reaction AoE
- **Indestructible** — Can't be broken, shapes the puzzle
- **Golden** — 1 hit, 500 points, always drops a power-up

### ⚡ 8 Power-Ups
Multi-Ball, Wide Paddle, Laser, Shield, Magnet, Slow-Mo, Fireball (pass-through), Mega Ball (3x size)

### 🏆 96 Achievements
Ranging from beginner milestones to mastery challenges: combo streaks, boss defeats, speed clears, survival endurance, career totals, mode completion, and more.

### 🎯 4 Boss Levels
Moving brick formations at levels 12, 24, 36, and 48 — each with unique movement patterns (horizontal, vertical, circular, figure-8).

### 📈 Progression System
- 50 player levels with XP scaling
- 8 ball skins and 7 paddle skins (unlocked through leveling)
- Career stats tracking: games played, bricks destroyed, combos, play time, per-mode best scores
- 3 challenge modifiers with XP multiplier bonuses
- Campaign completion tracking

### 🕹️ Controls
| Action | Browser | VR |
|--------|---------|-----|
| Move paddle | Mouse / A/D / ←/→ | Right controller position |
| Fire / Release magnet | Click | Trigger |
| Dash | Shift | — |
| Slam | X | — |
| Pause | Escape | — |

### 🎨 Visual Design
- 8 arena themes (Neon Holodeck, Crimson Grid, Toxic Neon, Ultra Violet, Solar Blaze, Frozen Abyss, Void Pulse, Emerald Matrix)
- Ring explosion VFX on brick destruction
- Brick entry elastic animations per level
- Brick damage visualization (darkening, roughness, edge fade)
- Brick glow pulsing (golden and explosive types)
- Combo visual escalation with paddle glow
- Screen shake on impacts
- Paddle dash trail particles
- Dynamic ball trails with skin-specific colors
- Wireframe holodeck environment with floating decorations
- Ambient particle system

### 🔊 Audio
- Procedural Web Audio engine — no audio file dependencies
- Arpeggiator music system with chord progression
- 15+ distinct SFX: brick hit/destroy, explosion, paddle, wall bounce, power-up, ball lost, level complete, game over, boss intro/defeat, laser, shield block, countdown, achievement
- Per-category volume controls (Master, SFX, Music)

### 🖥️ Spatial UI (PanelUI)
All 22 UI panels use IWSDK's native PanelUI system (`.uikitml` templates) — zero HTML DOM overlays:

`title` · `modeselect` · `difficulty` · `hud` · `pause` · `gameover` · `levelcomplete` · `leaderboard` · `achievements` · `settings` · `help` · `toast` · `countdown` · `profile` · `ballskins` · `paddleskins` · `levelup` · `modifiers` · `powerups` · `victory` · `tutorial` · `practiceselect`

Head-locked HUD panels (score, combo, active power-ups, wave counter) follow the player's gaze in VR via the `Follower` component.

## Tech Stack

- **IWSDK 0.4.1** — dual-runtime WebXR framework (VR + browser)
- **Three.js** — 3D rendering (via IWSDK)
- **PanelUI** — spatial UI system with `.uikitml` templates
- **Web Audio API** — procedural sound synthesis
- **TypeScript** — full type safety
- **Vite** — build and dev server
- **GitHub Pages** — hosting

## Development

```bash
npm install
npx iwsdk dev up        # Start dev server with XR emulation
npm run build           # Production build
```

## Stats

- **4,895+ lines** of source code (4,254 TypeScript + 641 uikitml)
- **22 PanelUI templates** — zero HTML DOM
- **48 handcrafted levels** across 4 themed zones
- **4 boss encounters** with unique movement patterns
- **96 achievements** across all modes and milestones
- **8 arena themes** · 8 ball skins · 7 paddle skins
- **50-level XP progression** with unlock rewards

## License

MIT

---

Built with [IWSDK](https://iwsdk.dev) by [ellyz2426](https://github.com/ellyz2426)

{"content":"# Latest Build Summary

## Neon Breaker VR — Build #23 (AM, 2026-06-01)

**Status:** ✅ Complete (360 min, 8 rounds)
**Genre:** Brick-breaking arcade — first in the portfolio
**Repo:** https://github.com/ellyz2426/neon-breaker
**Live:** https://ellyz2426.github.io/neon-breaker/

### Final Stats
- **30 files** | **4,924 lines** (4,274 TS + 650 uikitml)
- **22 PanelUI templates** — zero HTML DOM
- **96 achievements**
- **48 handcrafted levels** across 4 zones + 4 boss levels
- **8 game modes**: Classic, Endless, Time Attack, Zen, Daily Challenge, Survival, Practice, Boss Rush
- **8 power-ups**: Multi-Ball, Wide Paddle, Laser, Shield, Magnet, Slow-Mo, Fireball, Mega Ball
- **6 brick types**: Normal, Tough, Armored, Explosive, Indestructible, Golden
- **50-level XP progression** with ball/paddle skin unlocks
- **8 arena themes** | **8 ball skins** | **7 paddle skins**
- **3 challenge modifiers** with XP multiplier bonuses
- **Paddle abilities**: Dash (Shift) and Slam (X) with dedicated SFX

### Round-by-Round Summary
1. **R1 (45m):** Core physics, 12 levels, 4 modes, 8 power-ups, 20 achievements, holodeck env, deployed
2. **R2 (45m):** XP system (50 levels), profile, 24 levels, ball/paddle skins, daily challenge, 40 achievements
3. **R3 (45m):** Zone 3 (36 levels), 3 bosses, challenge modifiers, screen shake, arpeggiator music, 50 achievements
4. **R4 (45m):** Campaign victory screen, 8 arena themes, 60 achievements, active powerup HUD, combo escalation
5. **R5 (45m):** Survival mode, Practice mode, Mega Ball, tutorial, 75 achievements, comprehensive README
6. **R6 (45m):** Zone 4 (48 levels), boss 4 (figure-8), brick entry animations, paddle dash/slam, glow pulsing, 86 achievements
7. **R7 (45m):** Boss Rush mode, ring explosion VFX, brick damage visualization, 96 achievements, enhanced profile stats
8. **R8 (45m):** Comprehensive README rewrite, audio polish (dash/slam/combo SFX), enhanced help/tutorial

### Key Technical Highlights
- Full 8/8 rounds — strongest completion since Neon Marble (#17) and Neon Paddle (#22)
- All 22 UI panels use PanelUI spatial UI — zero HTML DOM overlays
- Procedural Web Audio with arpeggiator, 18+ distinct SFX, no audio file deps
- Dual-runtime (VR + browser) with XR controller support
- Ring explosion VFX adds satisfying visual feedback to every brick break
- Brick damage visualization gives multi-hit bricks clear damage states
- Boss Rush mode adds endgame challenge with 2x XP reward
","path":"workspace/iwsdk-daily-builds/latest-build-summary.md"}
